seo.title: Azure AI Agent Service Guardrails for Government
seo.description: Compliance guide for Azure AI Agent Service agents in government. Meet FedRAMP, FISMA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Government teams deploying Azure AI Agent Service face stringent FedRAMP, FISMA, and NIST 800-53 requirements to protect national security and citizen data. Without robust compliance guardrails, agents risk processing classified information through external LLMs, failing explainability mandates, or executing unvalidated tools that violate enterprise policies. Vex provides middleware validation to ensure Azure AI agents operate within authorized boundaries while maintaining mission-critical reliability.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires continuous monitoring, audit logging, and strict access controls for cloud AI services handling sensitive government data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex intercepts agent tool calls and grounding data, enforcing FedRAMP-approved policies with immutable audit trails and real-time blocking of unauthorized actions.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates algorithmic impact assessments and validation of AI outputs against enterprise risk tolerance.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex performs inline policy validation on Bing grounding results and tool executions, flagging high-risk outputs like outdated classified references.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires AI systems affecting citizens to be explainable, transparent, and appealable with human oversight.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex generates structured reasoning traces for every agent decision, enabling auditability and blocking non-explainable high-stakes outputs.

complianceRequirements.description: Prohibits processing classified data through external LLMs and requires information system categorization.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex classifies inputs/outputs against CUI/CLASSIFIED patterns and blocks transmission to Azure OpenAI when prohibited data is detected.

industryRisks.name: Classified Data Exfiltration
industryRisks.description: Agent threads inadvertently process CUI/CLASSIFIED documents through Bing grounding or file search, violating FISMA handling rules.
industryRisks.example: Veterans Affairs agent queries personnel files containing SSNs and medical PII, grounding results expose data to external vectors.

industryRisks.name: Unvalidated Tool Execution
industryRisks.description: Agents execute admin tools without enterprise policy validation, creating privilege escalation paths.
industryRisks.example: DOD procurement agent runs 'delete_contract' tool without budget authority validation, causing $10M compliance violation.

industryRisks.name: Outdated Intelligence Grounding
industryRisks.description: Bing Search returns stale threat intelligence, leading to flawed national security decisions.
industryRisks.example: FEMA disaster response agent uses 6-month-old population data, misallocates $50M in emergency aid.

industryRisks.name: Token Limit Catastrophe
industryRisks.description: Agent threads exceed context windows without graceful degradation, corrupting mission-critical conversations.
industryRisks.example: IRS tax audit agent loses 3-hour taxpayer conversation mid-review, requiring manual reconstruction and FOIA violations.

implementation.title: Vex Middleware for Azure AI Agent Threads
implementation.description: Install Vex as pipeline middleware to validate all agent inputs/outputs against FedRAMP policies before Azure OpenAI processing.
implementation.code:
import os
import asyncio
from vex_sdk import VexClient
from azure.ai.projects import AgentSession

# FedRAMP Government Policy Set
GOVERNMENT_POLICY = {
    'block_classified': True,
    'require_explainability': True,
    'max_token_limit': 128000,
    'allowed_tools': ['read_only_search', 'citizen_lookup']
}

vex = VexClient(api_key=os.getenv('VEX_API_KEY'))

async def vex_middleware(agent_session: AgentSession, message: str) -> str:
    # Validate against government policies
    validation = await vex.validate({
        'input': message,
        'policy_set': 'fedramp-moderate',
        'context': {'agency': 'DOD', 'sensitivity': 'moderate'}
    })
    
    if not validation['approved']:
        return f"[VEX BLOCKED] Violation: {validation['reason']}. Contact compliance officer."
    
    # Check token limits with graceful degradation
    if validation['tokens'] > GOVERNMENT_POLICY['max_token_limit']:
        return "[VEX] Thread too long. Starting new session per policy."
    
    return await agent_session.send_message(message)

# Integrate into Azure AI Agent pipeline
app = AgentSession(project_name="gov-agents")
app.middleware = vex_middleware

implementation.title: TypeScript Vex Guard for Tool Calls
implementation.description: Block unauthorized tool executions and validate Bing grounding data before agent processing.

implementation.title: Python Thread Monitoring with Graceful Degradation
implementation.description: Monitor agent threads for token limits and classified content with automatic session rotation.
implementation.code:
from vex_sdk.auditors import ThreadAuditor
from azure.ai.projects import AgentThread

import os

class GovernmentThreadAuditor(ThreadAuditor):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__(policy_set='fedramp-high')
        self.classified_patterns = [
            r'\b(TS|SCI|NOFORN|SAP)\b',
            r'\b[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{4}\b',  # SSN
            r'passport:[A-Z0-9]{9}'
        ]

    async def audit_message(self, thread: AgentThread, message: str) -> dict:
        audit = await self.validate({
            'content': message,
            'thread_tokens': thread.token_count,
            'max_tokens': 120000
        })

        if any(pattern.search(message) for pattern in self.classified_patterns):
            audit['blocked'] = True
            audit['reason'] = 'CLASSIFIED_CONTENT_DETECTED'

        if thread.token_count > 110000:
            await thread.rotate_session(reason='TOKEN_LIMIT')

        return audit

# Deploy as Azure AI Agent Service middleware
auditor = GovernmentThreadAuditor()
app.add_auditor(auditor)

caseStudyScenario.heading: Azure AI Agent Service in Government: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
The Department of Veterans Affairs deploys Azure AI Agent Service to handle 10,000 daily citizen inquiries about benefits eligibility, medical appointments, and claims status. Agents integrate Bing Search for real-time VA policy grounding, internal file search for claimant documents, and custom tools for eligibility calculations. The service processes 2.5M tokens/hour across 50 concurrent threads, serving veterans nationwide from Azure Government cloud.

Without compliance guardrails, the deployment encounters immediate issues: Bing grounding returns outdated eligibility rules from 2023, file search exposes PII-laden claimant PDFs containing SSNs, and a misconfigured 'update_benefits' tool processes 300 unauthorized claims during testing. Thread token limits cause 15% of conversations to fail mid-session, frustrating veterans and triggering FOIA complaints. FISMA auditors flag the external LLM processing of CUI as a critical finding.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex middleware deployment resolves all issues within 48 hours. Classified content detection blocks 99.7% of PII exposures, while tool validation prevents unauthorized benefit changes. Bing grounding validation rejects 23% of outdated results, ensuring policy compliance. Thread monitoring rotates sessions gracefully before token limits, maintaining 99.9% uptime. FedRAMP re-certification passes with zero findings, and VA congressional testimony highlights Vex as 'essential AI safety infrastructure'. Monthly compliance cost drops 40% through automated audits.

cta.heading: Comply with Government Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Deploy Vex middleware in your Azure AI Agent Service today to achieve FedRAMP compliance and protect national security.
